article.title: Stripe Onsite Interviews Experience
article.content:
Hey folks, I’ve my Stripe onsites coming up and chose C++ as my main language.
I heard Stripe doesn’t focus much on DSA but has tricky rounds like API Integration and Bug Bash.

Anyone who’s been through it 

How tough are these rounds really?

Any prep tips or resources (especially for C++)?

Would really appreciate any help! 

Update: Took Python as my preferred language.

Round 1 – Programming: Solved 3 parts; nothing too fancy, and the code wasn’t particularly clean.
Expected Outcome: Weak Yes / No

Round 2 – Bug Squash: This didn’t go well. My debugger wasn’t working — managed to fix one bug and explained my approach for the second as best as I could.
Expected Outcome: No / Strong No

Round 3 – Managerial Round: Discussed my current project in depth, but the interviewer didn’t seem too impressed.
Expected Outcome: No

Round 4 – Integration Round: The interviewer was friendly and encouraging. Completed 3 parts — almost ran out of time, but they gave me an extra few minutes to debug the last one. My code was a bit messy though.
Expected Outcome: Soft Yes / No

Overall:
I genuinely enjoyed interviewing with Stripe and loved the entire process just wish I had performed a bit better.


Current TC : 39L 
YOE : 2.3

post.content:
Hi I have been through the API Integration Round but got rejected there.
There were 5 subtasks to be done within an hour. 
The first one was - take a json file as input and print some values from the json file. This was easy and I was able to do it.
The second one was - I need to send an HTTP POST request and receive some image ig and all that stuff. 
I failed in solving the second task as I never worked with APIs before and had no clue what to do.  And so, I don't know what the further tasks were. 

I would suggest go through c++ libraries like libcurl, json, fstream, etc. for this Integration Round.
The HTTP requests were probably only accessible with libcurl in my case.

For programming round, I practiced the Design questions in leetcode, wrote the codes with proper variable names and comments. This round I was able to clear :)

My Role : Software Engineer Intern, 6 months

P.S. The Bug Bash round wasn't there for me as it was not for Intern roles. Looking at your experience, I guess they will have a System Design round added too. 
All the best :) This was all I could help.

A good thing is you are free to go through the internet during the interview. Only LLM models are not allowed to be used.

Hi I haven't been through the rounds but I've heard there is a bug bounty sort of round in which you need to find bugs in the code base and fix them . If you use Low level languages like c++ / c#, it could be a little challenging to debug the code-base unless your current work experience is also in C++ Codebase.

If you've experience with Java or Python , you can consider choosing them for this round.

All the very best !!
Worst idea to go with c++ or java
I think there are some good Leetcode posts on the same already, you can refer them once if not done
Congrats OP! Can you share how you opportunity?
